title: Smoky Silky Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and aromatic North African stew featuring tender lamb, unwaxed lemons, and a blend of warming spices, perfect for special occasions or cozy nights in. This gluten-free-friendly dish is sure to please a crowd with its deep, smoky flavors.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are softened and lightly caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.

Add the sliced preserved lemons, chicken broth, and browned lamb. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
